records.title: Canonical queue lags additive queue-update journal
records.summary: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json still ends Frontier Research at R-004 and does not contain the latest Cloudflare invocation-receipt improvement, while additive queue-update commits record R-006 and a newer C-001 state. The repository therefore currently has two state layers: a stale canonical snapshot and a newer append-only operational journal. Treating the canonical file alone as current would silently discard completed work; treating additive records alone as canonical would bypass shared-state materialization rules.
contradicts: Any interpretation that operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone is the complete current state
records.falsification_or_review_route: Materialize all additive operations/queue-updates records into a new canonical queue through an authenticated, replay-protected writer; then compare item IDs, dependencies, status, commit references, and timestamps. Resolve this contradiction only when the canonical snapshot provably includes or explicitly rejects every later journal entry.

records.title: Operational state requires snapshot plus append-only journal semantics
records.summary: The safest continuity model is not last-write-wins. ACTIVE_QUEUE.json should be treated as a materialized snapshot, while operations/queue-updates provides an append-only journal whose entries retain team ownership, evidence, and historical order. A protected materializer should merge only validated entries and emit a receipt linking the prior snapshot, accepted journal entries, resulting snapshot digest, workflow identity, and replay key.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Reject this inferred design if repository rules explicitly define additive queue-update files as non-authoritative notes, or if a protected materializer already exists and proves a different merge model. Otherwise test it by replaying the journal from the indexed canonical blob and checking deterministic output under reordered, duplicated, malformed, cross-owner, and stale entries.

records.title: No protected queue materialization receipt has been observed
records.summary: Cloudflare and Frontier artifacts define workflow identity, signature verification, invocation receipts, and replay-key boundaries, but this run found no evidence that those controls have materialized the additive queue journal into ACTIVE_QUEUE.json. The implementation path exists; the authoritative state transition remains unproven.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Resolve only with a retained materialization receipt containing the exact prior ACTIVE_QUEUE blob SHA, accepted queue-update blob digests, deterministic resulting queue digest, authenticated workflow identity, replay-key disposition, and resulting commit SHA.

registry_reason: Chat Archaeologist, Decision Historian, Contradiction Resolver, Language Evolution Mapper, and Privacy Boundary Auditor already cover the distinct responsibilities used in this slice.
